characters.name: Adam Galardner
characters.description: Adam GalardnerBiographyBirth1972-1973GenderMaleRomancesJennifer KeatonResidenceColumbus, Ohio, United StatesSeries InformationSeasons4Episodes1Appearance4.04:"Designated Hitter"Portrayed byNoah Hathaway Adam Galardneris a classmate ofJennifer Keaton. InBringing Up Baby, Jennifer has a crush on Adam and wants to ask him to the sixth grade dance, but is unsure if he likes her back. She is elated when Chrissie finds out that Adam does like Jennifer, but dismayed when she learns that he's getting braces and a retainer (although when he appears inDesignated Hitterhe doesn't appear to have braces).

characters.name: Alex P. Keaton
characters.description: Alex P. KeatonBiographyAliasesAlex(by everyone)Gandhi(by callers inHotline Fever)BirthJuly[1]1965 or 1966StatusSingleGenderMaleRelativesElyse Keaton(mother)Steven Keaton(father)Mallory Keaton(sister)Jennifer Keaton(sister)Andrew Keaton(brother)June(cousin)Regina(cousin)[2]Molly(aunt)[3]Ethel(aunt)[4]Helen(aunt)[5]Eddie(uncle)Rhoda(aunt)[6]Charlie(uncle)[7]Miriam(aunt)[8]RomancesRachel Miller(ex-girlfriend)Ellen Reed(ex-girlfriend)Lauren Miller(ex-girlfriend)Marty Brodie(ex-girlfriend)OccupationInvestment bankerResidenceNew York, New York, United StatesSeries InformationSeasons1•2•3•4•5•6•7Episodes176First1.01:"Pilot"Last7.26:"Alex Doesn't Live Here Anymore: Part 2"Portrayed byMichael J. Fox Alexander P. "Alex "Keatonis a fictional character on the American television sitcomFamily Ties,which aired on NBC for seven seasons, from 1982 to 1989. Michael J. Fox added the middle initial himself during an audition for the role, the creators liked it and so the name stuck.Family Tiesreflected the move in the United States away from the cultural liberalism of the 1960s and 1970s to the conservatism of the 1980s. This was particularly expressed through the relationship between Young Republican Alex (Michael J. Fox) and his hippie parents, Elyse and Steven Keaton (Meredith Baxter and Michael Gross). American president Ronald Reagan once stated thatFamily Tieswas his favorite television show. Alex himself embraces the concept of "Reaganomics." Alex is the oldest child of Elyse and Steven Keaton (Meredith Baxter-Birney and Michael Gross), who were Baby Boomers and Democrats during the early years of the Reagan administration. Married in 1964, Elyse, an independent architect, and Steven, a manager in a local public television station, were hippies during the 1960s. According to the episodeA Christmas Storyin season one, Alex was born in 1965 while his parents were on assignment in Africa; however, some other episodes give ages for Alex that suggest his birth year as 1966 instead, andA Tale of Two Cities: Part 2mentions him being three in April 1968. When he was a baby, his parents nicknamed him Moon Muffin[9]and Moonbeam and dressed him in love beads and tie-dyed pajamas.[10] Alex is smart and attractive and has a passion for economics and wealth. In particular, he is a proponent of supply-side economics. His heroes (seeFamous Conservatives) are Richard Nixon (going so far as to have a lunchbox with Nixon's likeness), William F. Buckley, Jr., Ronald Reagan, and Milton Friedman. His favorite television show isWall $treet Weekand he is an avid reader ofThe Wall Street Journal. He also enjoys music of the big band and swing era, but secretly enjoys rock music (as seen in the episode, "A, My Name is Alex"). Even though Alex tends to favor conservatism in all forms, and tends to come off as rather obnoxious at times when discussing his views, he is in actually a very kind and caring person who is always willing to lend an ear and offer his advice whenever there's a
characters.personality_section: Alex is smart and attractive and has a passion for economics and wealth. In particular, he is a proponent of supply-side economics. His heroes (seeFamous Conservatives) are Richard Nixon (going so far as to have a lunchbox with Nixon's likeness), William F. Buckley, Jr., Ronald Reagan, and Milton Friedman. His favorite television show isWall $treet Weekand he is an avid reader ofThe Wall Street Journal. He also enjoys music of the big band and swing era, but secretly enjoys rock music (as seen in the episode, "A, My Name is Alex"). Even though Alex tends to favor conservatism in all forms, and tends to come off as rather obnoxious at times when discussing his views, he is in actually a very kind and caring person who is always willing to lend an ear and offer his advice whenever there's a problem, even though the latter is dubious at best. A major running gag is Alex's obsession with eventually becoming rich and leaving his mundane middle-class existence behind. Despite this, a major theme throughout the series is how he ultimately choses his family and friends over several major opportunities to get further ahead: These decisions ultimately mean very little in the end, as he does eventually earn everything he desires despite all of these willful setbacks that he endures.

Even though Alex tends to favor conservatism in all forms, and tends to come off as rather obnoxious at times when discussing his views, he is in actually a very kind and caring person who is always willing to lend an ear and offer his advice whenever there's a problem, even though the latter is dubious at best. characters.description: AliceBiographyGenderFemaleSeries InformationSeasons4Episodes1Appearance4.22:"My Buddy"Portrayed byAnne Marie McEvoy Aliceis a Sunflower Girl who declares that boys are only interested in sex and asksSteven Keatonif he was interested in sex when he was a boy.

characters.name: Allison King
characters.description: Allison KingBiographyGenderFemaleSeries InformationSeasons5Episodes1Appearance5.12:"My Mother, My Friend"Portrayed byRobin Morse Allison Kingis a member ofMallory Keaton's sorority. She is majoring in architecture atGrant College. She and Mallory meet in registration, since both their last names start with K. They become good friends. Allison goes to theKeaton family's house, where she meetsElyse Keatonand strikes up a friendship with her. Allison interviews Elyse for a paper in her city planning class (for which she gets an A) and goes to a museum exhibit and an architecture symposium with her. Mallory is jealous of how much attention her mother is paying to Allison. Elyse and Allison decide to hold one of the sorority meetings at the Keaton house and make it a party in honor of Allison. At the party, Allison teaches Elyse to slam spin. After a while, Mallory angrily accuses Elyse of being overly involved with her life and her friends, breaks down crying, and runs out of the house.

characters.name: Amy Sussman
characters.description: Amy SussmanBiographyStatusIn a relationshipGenderFemaleRomancesSkippy Handelman(ex-boyfriend)Series InformationSeasons5•6Episodes2First5.25:"Love Me Do"Last6.11:"Citizen Keaton"Portrayed bySonia Curtis Amy Sussmanis a premed student atGrant College. She takes a psychology class withMallory Keaton. Through Mallory she meetsSkippy Handelman, who asks her on a date. Amy accepts, becoming the first girl who's ever returned Skippy's interest. Amy enjoys the date, even after Skippy accidentally yanks the tablecloth onto the floor. The two start going out regularly. Eventually Amy invites Skippy to her dorm room while her roommate, Mary Beth, is visiting her parents in Alaska. Skippy tries to create romantic mood lighting by draping a piece of cloth over a lamp. It seems to work until the lamp catches fire. Later that night, they meet up again at the Keatons' house, where they affirm their love for each other. InCitizen Keaton, Amy serves as treasurer forMallory Keaton's campaign for school president.

characters.name: Andrea Neff
characters.description: Andrea NeffBiographyBirthlate 1960sStatusSingleGenderFemaleRomancesBilly(ex-boyfriend)Series InformationSeasons6Episodes1Appearance6.03:"Dear Mallory"Portrayed byJane Adams Andrea Neffis a woman who writes toMallory Keaton's advice column. Throughout her adolescence, boys were totally uninterested in her. Recently she's managed to get a boyfriend, for the first time in her life. When a friend tells her that her boyfriend Billy is seeing another woman, Andrea writes to Mallory for advice. Mallory advises her to confront him to find out if he's cheating, and to cut him loose if he is. Andrea confronts Billy, who denies cheating, but is so offended that she asked the question that he says he never wants to see her again. After theKeaton family's address is published in the Columbus Shoppers' Guide, Andrea is one of many people who go to their house to ask Mallory for advice in person. Andrea tells Mallory that her boyfriend dumped her, says she'll never have a boyfriend again, and tells Mallory that it's easy for someone as pretty as Mallory to say that there are other fish in the sea, but that Andrea is plain and will be single forever. Then she runs out of the house. Mallory chases her down, but can do nothing but apologize.

characters.name: Andrew
characters.description: AndrewBiographyGenderMaleRelativesAndrew(son)OccupationRoom service waiterResidenceWashington, D.C., United StatesSeries InformationSeasons4Episodes1Appearance4.16:"Nothing But a Man"Portrayed byRobert Costanzo Andrewis the man who delivers room service toSteven KeatonandJustin Phillips' hotel room in Washington, D.C. He has a brief conversation with Steven Keaton about the fact that they both have a son named Andrew. Andrew Jr. is thirty-two, has purple hair, and changes jobs (or claims to) every week. The last time he talked to his father, he said he was working as a record producer in New York.

characters.name: Arlene Handelman
characters.description: Arlene HandelmanBiographyGenderFemaleRelativesHarry Handelman(father)Rose Handelman(mother)Skippy Handelman(brother)ResidenceColumbus, Ohio, United StatesSeries InformationSeasons2Episodes1Appearance2.08:"Batter Up"Portrayed byTanya Fenmore Arlene Handelmanis the younger sister ofSkippy Handelman. Arlene resembles Skippy in both appearance and personality even though they aren't biologically related. It's unclear whether she's adopted, too. Her family has pet guinea pigs[1]and two turtles.[2]Skippy once saved her life through "simple reflexes," although we never learn the details.[3]

characters.name: Arthur Press
characters.description: Arthur PressBiographyStatusSingleGenderMaleRomancesElizabeth(ex-wife)OccupationWKSSeries InformationEpisodes1Portrayed byDavid Spielberg"Uncle" Arthur Pressis a family friend of the Keatons who appears inGive Your Uncle Arthur a Kiss. Arthur has worked withSteven KeatonatWKSfor fourteen years, during which he greenlit Sesame Street.  According to Steven, his name is synonymous with WKS.  Arthur is a close friend of Steven, whose kids call him Uncle Arthur.  He's knownMallorysince she was a baby. Arthur used to be married to a woman named Elizabeth, but they split up, causing Arthur to go through a midlife crisis.

At a sorority party at theKeaton family's house, she invitesElyse Keatonto join the sorority and asks her how she manages to find time for work and a family, since Becky rarely has time to date.

characters.description: Bernie PackerBiographyGenderMaleSeries InformationSeasons6Episodes1Appearance6.16:"Anniversary Waltz"Portrayed byHoward Renensland Bernie Packeris a friend ofElyse Keaton. Bernie owns a condo on Lake Erie. A few years ago, he did something really helpful forKate Donnelly.

characters.name: Bernie Packer
characters.description: Bernie PackerBiographyGenderMaleSeries InformationSeasons6Episodes1Appearance6.16:"Anniversary Waltz"Portrayed byHoward Renensland Bernie Packeris a friend ofElyse Keaton. Bernie owns a condo on Lake Erie. A few years ago, he did something really helpful forKate Donnelly.

characters.name: Bert Carlyle
characters.description: Bert CarlyleBiographyGenderMaleOccupationFBI agentSeries InformationSeasons1Episodes2First1.14:"The Fugitive: Part 1"Last1.15:"The Fugitive: Part 2"Portrayed byRichard Venture Agent Bert Carlyleis a member of the local office of the FBI.  He's the agent sent to the Keatons' house to arrestNed Donnellyfor embezzlement.  He brings a copy of the FBI's file onSteven KeatonandElyse Keaton, which he lets them read.  He warns the Keatons that if they know where Ned is but don't tell him, they'll go to jail.  The Keatons pretend not to know anything, but that night Carlyle follows Alex and Ned to the airport where Ned tries to flee the state.  He finds Alex, but Ned runs away before he can arrest him.  The next day Carlyle returns to the Keatons' house withRob Peterson.  This time Ned turns himself in, and Carlyle arrests him.

characters.name: Bert Carlyle
characters.description: Bert CarlyleBiographyGenderMaleOccupationFBI agentSeries InformationSeasons1Episodes2First1.14:"The Fugitive: Part 1"Last1.15:"The Fugitive: Part 2"Portrayed byRichard Venture Agent Bert Carlyleis a member of the local office of the FBI.  He's the agent sent to the Keatons' house to arrestNed Donnellyfor embezzlement.  He brings a copy of the FBI's file onSteven KeatonandElyse Keaton, which he lets them read.  He warns the Keatons that if they know where Ned is but don't tell him, they'll go to jail.  The Keatons pretend not to know anything, but that night Carlyle follows Alex and Ned to the airport where Ned tries to flee the state.  He finds Alex, but Ned runs away before he can arrest him.  The next day Carlyle returns to the Keatons' house withRob Peterson.  This time Ned turns himself in, and Carlyle arrests him.

characters.name: Beth Davis
characters.description: Beth DavisBiographyStatusMarriedGenderFemaleRelativesMichelle Davis(daughter)RomancesRay Davis(husband)Series InformationSeasons6Episodes1Appearance6.17:"Miracle in Columbus"Portrayed byLee Garlington Beth Davisis the mother ofMichelle Davisand the wife ofRay Davis. Ray works as a traveling salesman, but work has been bad during 1987. A few days before Christmas, he's forced to go to Fargo on business. He expects to spend Christmas away from his family. Beth takes Michelle to the mall, where they meetAlex P. Keaton, who is working as a Santa. Michelle asks Alex to bring her daddy home for Christmas, but Alex says he can't do that. Beth explains the family's problem to Alex. Alex, moved, invites Beth and Michelle to theKeaton family's Christmas Eve party. Ray also shows up to the party, having arrived home for Christmas thanks to a miracle from Santa Claus.

characters.name: Beth Davis
characters.description: Beth DavisBiographyStatusMarriedGenderFemaleRelativesMichelle Davis(daughter)RomancesRay Davis(husband)Series InformationSeasons6Episodes1Appearance6.17:"Miracle in Columbus"Portrayed byLee Garlington Beth Davisis the mother ofMichelle Davisand the wife ofRay Davis. Ray works as a traveling salesman, but work has been bad during 1987. A few days before Christmas, he's forced to go to Fargo on business. He expects to spend Christmas away from his family. Beth takes Michelle to the mall, where they meetAlex P. Keaton, who is working as a Santa. Michelle asks Alex to bring her daddy home for Christmas, but Alex says he can't do that. Beth explains the family's problem to Alex. Alex, moved, invites Beth and Michelle to theKeaton family's Christmas Eve party. Ray also shows up to the party, having arrived home for Christmas thanks to a miracle from Santa Claus.

characters.name: Beth Hooper
characters.description: Beth HooperBiographyBirth1972-1973StatusSingleGenderFemaleResidenceColumbus, Ohio, United StatesSeries InformationSeasons5Episodes2First5.28:"It's My Party: Part 1"Last5.29:"It's My Party: Part 2"Portrayed byNicole Nourmand Beth Hooperis a friend and soccer teammate ofJennifer Keaton. She is seen by the popular kids as "the geek of all time" and is never invited to any parties except Jennifer's. However, Jennifer doesn't invite her to her thirteenth birthday party because she andChrissyare trying to become cool. Three weeks after the party, Beth goes to Jennifer's house, where a party full of cool kids is raging.Stacie Connorstries to kick Beth out for not being cool enough, but Jennifer stands up for Beth and instead kicks everyone else out, except for Chrissy. Jennifer, Chrissy, and Beth decide to start their own cool group, because as far as Beth is concerned, she's always been cool.

characters.description: Betty BoswellBiographyStatusMarriedGenderFemaleRelativesTimmy Boswell(son)RomancesRalph Boswell(husband)Series InformationSeasons4Episodes1Appearance4.13:"The Disciple"Portrayed byCynthia Carle Betty Boswellis the mother of Timmy Boswell, a boy inAndrew Keaton's playgroup. Betty and her husband Ralph attend the contest for the Thomas Dewey Award.

characters.name: Bill Channing
characters.description: Bill ChanningBiographyStatusDivorcedGenderMaleOccupationArchitectResidenceColumbus, Ohio, United StatesSeries InformationSeasons2Episodes1Appearance1.22:"Working At It"Portrayed byWalter Olkewicz Bill Channingis one of the architects who works for the firm run byKaren Banks. Bill's parents made him go to college, even though he didn't want to. For his senior project, he designed a city of the future, large enough for 500,000 people. He has been married and divorced. He's currently unmarried and has no kids. He's fond of corny jokes.

characters.name: Bobby
characters.description: BobbyBiographyStatusIn a relationshipGenderMaleRomancesJanieSeries InformationSeasons7Episodes1Appearance7.04:"Beyond Therapy"Portrayed byStephen Baldwin Bobbyis a man who attendsDr. Davidson's therapy group with his girlfriendJanie. Bobby's problem is that he flies into a rage whenever Janie so much as looks at another guy.

characters.name: Bobby Cahill
characters.description: Bobby CahillBiographyGenderMaleOccupationSenior stock boySeries InformationPortrayed byRandy Lowell Bobby Cahillis the senior stock boy in charge ofAlex P. Keaton's training at the Shop-A-Lot inDeath of a Grocer. Bobby is highly impressed by Alex's stocking abilities and thinks that he could be assistant manager of pet supplies within the year.
